observations: SCOPE OF THIS PASS: a full round-2 re-review (not a metadata-only delta), since revision 1 changed operative verification content (2 new entries, 1 fixed entry) and design_notes prose, not merely bookkeeping. All three round-0 HIGH findings were independently re-probed from scratch against the live repository, not trusted from the contract's own dry_run_proof narrative.
SCHEMA VALIDITY: `python3 -c "jsonschema.validate(...)"` re-run against the live revision-1 contract and schema/contract.schema.json: exits 0, schema-valid.
ARRAY LENGTH: parsed the live revision-1 'verification' array programmatically: exactly 26 entries, matching evidence_convention's own stated count (24 -> 26, +2 new entries). No off-by-one.

STRUCTURAL DIFF AGAINST THE EXACT REVISION-0 TEXT THIS EVALUATOR ORIGINALLY REVIEWED (reconstructed verbatim from this evaluator's own round-0 transcript, not re-derived from the generator's characterization): contract_id, feature_id, name, status, spec_ref, scope (files_create/files_modify, all 6 entries), estimated_lines (165), evidence_dir are all byte-identical (Python equality). design_notes.nature_of_this_feature and design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts are byte-identical. non_goals: 12 entries in both revisions; exactly ONE differs (non_goals[0]), updated to reflect 035 now committed to HEAD and 036 now approved/implementing -- a pure concurrency-status-narrative update, not a scope change (the enumerated non-edited ecosystem/*.md paths and the dual-tolerance rationale are otherwise the same). design_notes.schema_design, concurrency_fence, and dry_run_proof are the three fields explicitly marked CORRECTED/REVISED (typo fix + the two structural fixes' narrative) -- consistent with the claimed change scope.

VERIFICATION ARRAY POSITIONAL MAPPING: mapped all 24 revision-0 entries onto their revision-1 positions (rev0[1..4]->rev1[1..4]; rev0[5..12]->rev1[6..13] (shifted +1 by the new entry inserted at rev1[5]); rev0[13..24]->rev1[15..26] (shifted +2 by the second new entry inserted at rev1[14])) and diffed 'command' text field-by-field using the exact command strings extracted programmatically from both the live revision-1 contract and this evaluator's own saved round-0 command files: the ONLY command that differs is rev0[24]->rev1[26] (the scope-guard fix, expected). All other 22 mapped commands are byte-identical, confirming no verification command was silently altered under cover of this revision. 'acceptance_test' prose differs only at rev0[4]->rev1[4] (re-labelled 'weak, schema-text-only sanity check' per this evaluator's own issues[2] finding) and rev0[24]->rev1[26] ('FIXED IN REVISION 1' framing) -- both expected, narrative-only changes.

FULL 26-ENTRY RE-RUN (all commands extracted programmatically from the live revision-1 contract, not retyped, executed from the repository root against the live pre-implementation tree, HEAD now at 1c8b07a with feature 036 fully committed): content-anchored entries (1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 17) correctly FAIL today (schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json and the fixtures are absent from both the working tree and HEAD; NIZAM.json and schema/README.md are unmodified). The anti-vacuity master proofs (2, 12, 16) and the regression/non-goal/scope/immutability guards (18-26) all currently PASS -- INCLUDING entry 26 (the previously-failing scope guard), which now passes cleanly.

FIX 1 RE-VERIFICATION (entry 26, scope guard): re-ran entry 26's literal, unmodified command against the live post-036-commit tree -- PASS (no longer fails). Separately re-ran the OLD (revision-0) allow-list form (omitting 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md') against the SAME live tree: it now ALSO passes, because feature 036's ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md and ecosystem/README.md changes are fully committed to HEAD (1c8b07a) and therefore no longer appear in `git status --porcelain` at all -- confirmed via a direct `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- . ':(exclude).agent'` re-run, which shows only 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' as a changed path outside .agent/. This means the contract's own dry_run_proof claim (12) that 'the prior, rejected form was independently reconfirmed to still FAIL' is now a time-relative claim that no longer reproduces exactly as stated (it was true when 036 was still mid-implementation/uncommitted, at the moment revision 1 was drafted, but 036 has since been committed) -- NON-BLOCKING: the fixed form (entry 26) is a superset-tolerant allow-list that correctly passes in BOTH the pre-commit and post-commit states, so the fix remains necessary-and-sufficient for the general case regardless of 036's exact commit timing, and no re-review action is required. Flagged here only for the record, not as a defect.

FIX 2 RE-VERIFICATION (NIZAM.json dual-surface, entries 13+14 pair): built three disposable scratch NIZAM.json variants from the real file (never written back) -- Variant A (schema appended to the top-level 'schemas' array ONLY), Variant B (schema appended to the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array ONLY), and a fully dual-surface-compliant variant. Re-ran entry 13's and entry 14's exact literal logic against all three: Variant A -> entry13 FAILS / entry14 PASSES; Variant B -> entry13 PASSES / entry14 FAILS; dual-compliant -> both PASS. The pair now fully and uniquely distinguishes all three cases; no single-surface edit can satisfy both entries simultaneously. The round-0 gap (a module-only edit passing everything) is closed.

FIX 3 RE-VERIFICATION (six-category loop, entry 5, the 'kill shot'): re-built the disposable STRICT scratch schema (schema_design as described: all six categories required + minItems:1) and the adversarial 'five-loose' scratch schema (only repository_references required/minItems:1, the other five typed-but-unconstrained) from the round-0 review, placed each alongside the three real-shaped fixtures in a disposable scratch repo root, and ran entry 5's EXACT, unmodified command body (re-extracted from the live revision-1 contract, not retyped) in each: against STRICT, all 12 probes (6 categories x {missing, empty}) correctly raise ValidationError -- entry 5 exits 0 (PASS). Against FIVE-LOOSE, exactly the 10 probes touching the five non-repository categories incorrectly validate (itemized: 'framework_references: missing-category unexpectedly validated; framework_references: empty-array unexpectedly validated; dependency_references: ...; ci_references: ...; planning_references: ...; evidence_references: ...' -- 10 items, repository_references contributing zero failures since it remains required/minItems:1 in the five-loose variant too) -- entry 5 exits 1 (GAPS FOUND, itemized), exactly as the contract's own dry_run_proof (10) claims. This is the load-bearing distinguishing mechanism the round-0 review found missing; independently reproduced, not trusted from the generator's narrative. No shared-state leakage between loop iterations was found (each of the 12 probes deep-copies the real base fixture independently).

FRESH-DEFECT SWEEP ON THE TWO NEW ENTRIES: entry 14 uses correct list membership (`in`) against d['schemas'], not equality -- no membership-vs-equality defect. Entry 5's `sys.exit('GAPS FOUND: ...')` / implicit fall-through-to-0-on-empty-failures-list gating was traced by hand and confirmed sound (non-empty failures -> exit 1 with itemized message; empty failures -> script completes normally, exit 0) -- not an ungated-echo-PASS defect. The scope-guard fix (entry 26) adds a single new exact-path allow-list token ('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md'); this carries the same prefix-match tolerance characteristic every other allow-listed token in this same entry already has (vlib_scope_guard matches on `path = allow` OR `path` prefixed by `allow`, an already-vetted, pre-existing property of the shared primitive, unchanged by this fix and applied consistently to every token in the list) -- not a new or fix-specific risk.

CONCURRENCY / PROBE-ISOLATION: confirmed live that feature 036 is now fully committed (`git log`: 1c8b07a 'feat(005): progress comparison protocol (feature 036)'), and feature 039 is 'proposed' (contract-drafting) with its own untracked .agent/contracts/039.json -- tolerated, not touched, throughout this review. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this review's probes shows only this feature's own contract/review artifacts and 039's own untracked contract, plus the pre-existing v0.6.0-release-notes.md; `git diff HEAD --stat` is empty. All scratch schemas, fixtures, and disposable NIZAM.json/version-probe copies used in this round-2 review live only under the session scratchpad directory, outside the repository tree; nothing under schema/, ecosystem/, tools/, docs/, or NIZAM.json in the real repository, and no other feature's .agent/contracts or .agent/qa artifact, was created, edited, or deleted at any point in this review. No edit was made to .agent/run_state.json.

TYPO FIX CONFIRMED: design_notes.schema_design's citation of schema/README.md's own house convention now reads 'Permits `additionalProperties` on extension points so consumer repositories can extend a shape without breaking validation, while still enforcing the required keys and enums that make an artifact machine-legible' -- re-read schema/README.md directly and confirmed this is now the verbatim, non-duplicated text (the round-0 'on extension points on extension points' duplication is gone).

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. All three HIGH-severity findings from the round-0 review (.agent/qa/037-contract-review.json) were independently re-probed from scratch against the live repository -- not trusted from the generator's dry_run_proof narrative -- and confirmed genuinely closed. (1) The scope-guard entry (now index 26) was re-run against the live tree, which now has feature 036 fully committed to HEAD (1c8b07a): it passes; the fix ('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' added to the allow-list) is a superset-tolerant, HEAD-relative construction that correctly handles both the pre-commit and post-commit states, so it remains sound regardless of exact concurrent-feature commit timing. (2) The NIZAM.json dual-surface gap is closed: a new entry (index 14) exactly checks top-level 'schemas' array membership, and re-probing three disposable scratch NIZAM.json variants (top-level-only, module-only, dual-compliant) against the entry-13/entry-14 pair confirms only the genuinely dual-surface-compliant edit satisfies both; either single-surface edit now fails exactly one of the two. (3) The six-category coverage gap is closed: a new instance-level entry (index 5) loops over all six Sec 3 categories against the real positive fixture, independently proving each category's top-level 'required' status (delete-in-turn) and minItems:1 (empty-in-turn); re-running this entry's exact command against a disposable STRICT scratch schema (12/12 probes correctly raise ValidationError, entry PASSES) and against the round-0 adversarial 'five-loose' scratch schema (10/12 probes incorrectly validate, itemized by category and condition, entry correctly FAILS) reproduces the generator's own claimed result and confirms the new entry is the load-bearing distinguishing mechanism, not a restatement of the still-present-but-now-correctly-relabelled bare substring check (index 4, 'weak sanity check'). A full structural diff against the exact revision-0 text this evaluator originally reviewed confirms scope, non_goals (except one concurrency-status-narrative sentence), estimated_lines, and 22 of 24 mapped verification commands are byte-identical; only the two new entries and the one fixed entry actually changed operative content, exactly matching the claimed change scope. No fresh defect was found in either new entry (correct membership semantics, correct exit-code gating, no cross-iteration state leakage). The citation-accuracy typo flagged in the round-0 review is confirmed corrected. Fit for implementation as-is.
